body:
Two inefficiencies:
1. summary() calls ready() per issue, which loads each dep from disk — O(n*d) reads. An in-memory set of closed IDs for the duration of the call would fix it.
2. add_note() rewrites the issue JSON just to bump updated_at, making every note commit touch two files. Consider leaving the issue JSON untouched for note-only operations.
title: Reduce unnecessary disk I/O in summary and add_note
